standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target

identifier_normalization.subject_id_role: raw patientid normalizes to subject_id and is used to search future local critical-care admissions
identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: not separately available in the current Amsterdam public mapping and retained as nullable context
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: raw admissionid is the Amsterdam ICU/MCU local admission identifier and normalizes to stay_id

database_mapping.source_grain: one row per Amsterdam ICU/MCU local admission record in admissions_core
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained row per Amsterdam ICU/MCU local admission with a nullable observed future ICU/MCU local-admission duration

target_scope.scope_note: This split identity intentionally includes Amsterdam MC-only rows; it must not be published as same-name std_days_to_next_icu_admission.

summary_build_translation.window_start_translation: start at admissions_core.dischargedat for the current Amsterdam ICU/MCU local admission
summary_build_translation.window_end_translation: end at the admittedat offset of the first later Amsterdam ICU/MCU local admission observed for the same patientid
summary_build_translation.qualifying_row_translation: every admissions_core row with location in (IC, MC, IC&MC, MC&IC), patientid, admissionid, and dischargedat is a candidate retained local admission row
summary_build_translation.selection_translation: sort admissions by patientid, admittedat, and admissionid; for each current admission, select the first later row for the same patient with admittedat strictly greater than current dischargedat
summary_build_translation.aggregation_translation: compute (next_later_admittedat - current_dischargedat) in days and round half away from zero to 3 decimal places
summary_build_translation.tie_break_translation: after enforcing strict post-discharge timing, use stable ordering by admittedat then admissionid for candidate future local admissions
summary_build_translation.no_source_row_translation: do not synthesize admissions outside admissions_core; retain the source local admission row with null duration when no later ICU/MCU local admission is observed
summary_build_translation.partial_window_translation: missing current dischargedat produces an unresolved status; missing future local admission means the retained value is null, not zero

representation_and_normalization.source_unit_expectation: raw millisecond offset interval converted to days

validation_contract.no_source_row_policy_check: admissions_core ICU/MCU local admissions are the target source rows; no additional null target rows are synthesized outside admissions_core
validation_contract.window_consistency_action: exclude overlapping, same-boundary, self-match, or nonpositive-gap candidates from the retained next-local-admission match
validation_contract.death_context_policy_check: dateofdeath may be retained as context flags but must not be used as the primary local-admission ordering rule in this opening mapping
notes: The retained target grain is Amsterdam ICU/MCU local admission, not hospital admission encounter.
The retained duration is computed from current admissions_core.dischargedat to the first later admissions_core.admittedat for the same patientid within the ICU/MCU location scope.
The retained value may be null; null means no later Amsterdam ICU/MCU local admission was observed or the current discharge boundary was unresolved.
This variable is not std_days_to_next_hospital_admission and does not prove hospital readmission semantics.
This variable intentionally includes MC-only rows and therefore remains split from same-name ICU-only std_days_to_next_icu_admission.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint reads this mapping spec and delegates to the current local reviewed implementation until a more fully spec-native runner replaces it.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_days_to_next_icu_mcu_admission outputs for AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path rather than side scripts or ad hoc rebuilds.
